leads.title: Engineer
leads.industry: IT Services & Consulting / Automotive Technology / Autonomous Vehicles
leads.ai_summary: NavAjna Technologies Private Limited is a Hyderabad-based IT services and automotive tech company founded in 2015, specializing in software modernization, L3-L5 autonomous vehicle R&D, computer vision, and product engineering. With ~31–99 employees and ~$2M annual revenue, it is bootstrapped but growing steadily at 14% revenue CAGR. The company holds co-developed IPs with major automotive OEMs and is an Intel technology partner. It is actively scaling its team in 2025. The lead, Shubham Mirashi, is listed as an 'Engineer' using a personal Gmail address, suggesting either an early-stage employee or limited corporate email access. No public LinkedIn profile found for this individual at NavAjna.

extra_data.recent_news_summary: NavAjna is actively hiring AI/ML and software engineering roles in 2025. The company collaborated with Intel to build 'Avanta AI', a conversational AI product using GAN models optimized via OpenVINO. Revenue CAGR of 14% and EBITDA CAGR of 105% (FY2024). Last AGM held September 2024. No external funding rounds identified.

With ~31–99 employees and ~$2M annual revenue, it is bootstrapped but growing steadily at 14% revenue CAGR. ec2d0805-e129-41cb-91f8-d09df9009a9a.next_best_action: Do NOT prioritize this individual contact for outreach. Instead, re-target the NavAjna account with a higher-authority contact. Use ZoomInfo or LinkedIn to identify the CTO, VP Engineering, or Delivery Head (likely one of the 2 directors: Uma Nair or Neha Jain per MCA filings). The account has real buying signals — active hiring, Intel partnership, AV R&D expansion — so the company is worth a nurture sequence. Reach out via corporate email (uma.nair@navajna.com is publicly listed on MCA/ClearTax filings) with a personalized message referencing their Avanta AI/OpenVINO work and AV scale-up challenge. If Shubham Mirashi must be engaged, treat him as a potential champion/influencer to navigate to the right decision-maker, not as a buyer.
ec2d0805-e129-41cb-91f8-d09df9009a9a.reasoning: FIRMOGRAPHIC (14/25): NavAjna is a real, active Hyderabad-based IT services and automotive tech company founded in 2015, confirmed bootstrapped with ~INR 2.21Cr (~$265K USD) revenue as of FY2024 — well below typical enterprise software deal thresholds. Employee count is firmly SMB-tier (31 confirmed by Tracxn Oct 2024; ZoomInfo estimates 50–99 suggesting some contractor/associate inflation). The industry fit (autonomous vehicles, computer vision, software modernization) is technically interesting — especially Intel OpenVINO usage and L3-L5 AV co-IPs with OEMs — but the company's limited revenue base (~$265K USD) severely constrains budget for new software purchases. Operates out of Hyderabad/Pune/Vadodara. Geographic fit is India SMB, not a high-ACV enterprise account. Score capped due to size/budget mismatch for most enterprise solutions. TITLE (5/25): 'Engineer' is a non-decision-making IC role with no budget authority whatsoever. No procurement influence, no departmental ownership. The personal Gmail address (shubhammirashi303@gmail.com) further erodes credibility — this is either a junior employee, intern, or someone probing independently without organizational sponsorship. No public LinkedIn profile was found for this individual at NavAjna, making it impossible to verify seniority or tenure. This is the single biggest disqualifier for this lead. INTENT (16/25): Genuine buying signals exist at the COMPANY level, not the individual level. ZoomInfo confirms active hiring in 2025 (AI/ML Trainee joined May 2025, Software Engineer and Sales Analytics Head joined Feb 2025). The Intel-Avanta AI collaboration and OpenVINO investment signal real technology appetite. A 105% EBITDA CAGR and 14% revenue CAGR show financial momentum. ZoomInfo also flagged a masked buying intent signal in the Business Services sector. However, these signals benefit a future, properly-targeted contact at NavAjna — not this specific lead. ENGAGEMENT (7/25): Lead was submitted manually via a personal Gmail with no verifiable LinkedIn profile, no phone number, and no corporate email. Engagement warmth is near-zero for this individual contact. There is no indication this person has decision-making authority, was referred, or self-identified a specific pain point. The account itself has some inbound signal but this contact is essentially an unqualified inbound.

ec2d0805-e129-41cb-91f8-d09df9009a9a.body: Hey Shubham — saw NavAjna's post about the Avanta AI build with Intel (the 9.4X FPS jump on OpenVINO was a great write-up). Working on AV and computer vision workloads at that scale, infra headroom must come up a lot. I work with engineering teams solving exactly that — would love to swap notes if it's relevant to what you're building.

ec2d0805-e129-41cb-91f8-d09df9009a9a.subject: NavAjna's Avanta AI + the infra cost of scaling GAN workloads
ec2d0805-e129-41cb-91f8-d09df9009a9a.body:
Hi Shubham,

I came across NavAjna's collaboration with Intel on Avanta AI — the write-up on pushing GAN model inference from 5 FPS to 47 FPS via OpenVINO was genuinely impressive engineering.

When teams reach that kind of performance milestone, the next challenge is usually running those workloads reliably at scale without infra costs spiralling — especially when you're also carrying L3–L5 AV R&D and onboarding new AI/ML engineers (noticed the recent hires on ZoomInfo).

We help engineering teams at companies like NavAjna manage scalable cloud infrastructure for compute-heavy workloads — computer vision, model inferencing, distributed pipelines — without needing a dedicated DevOps team to babysit it.

Worth a 20-minute conversation? Happy to work around your calendar.

Best,
[Your Name]

ec2d0805-e129-41cb-91f8-d09df9009a9a.subject: What teams similar to NavAjna typically save in compute costs
ec2d0805-e129-41cb-91f8-d09df9009a9a.body:
Hi Shubham,

Following up on my note from a couple of days ago.

To make this more concrete: engineering teams in the AV and computer vision space that we work with typically reduce cloud compute spend by 30–40% within the first 90 days — mainly through right-sizing GPU/CPU allocation for inference workloads and eliminating idle cluster time on Kubernetes environments.

Given NavAjna is running Kubernetes, Docker, and Kafka alongside active GAN model development, the opportunity is usually sitting in how inference jobs are scheduled and how dev/test environments are managed across Hyderabad, Pune, and Vadodara.

Not a big lift to validate — we usually do a free infra audit in a single session. Would that be useful right now, or is there a better stakeholder at NavAjna to loop in?

Best,
[Your Name]

ec2d0805-e129-41cb-91f8-d09df9009a9a.subject: Closing the loop
ec2d0805-e129-41cb-91f8-d09df9009a9a.body:
Hi Shubham,

I'll keep this short — I've reached out a couple of times referencing NavAjna's AV and AI work, and I don't want to keep pinging if the timing is off or I'm talking to the wrong person.

If infra scalability for your computer vision or AV workloads becomes a priority later in the year, I'm easy to find. And if there's someone on the engineering or ops side at NavAjna who owns that conversation, happy to be pointed their way.

Wishing the team well with the Avanta AI roadmap — genuinely interesting work.

[Your Name]
